target: SEC-4.1 TLDR and prose fixes: remove unsupported lambda_g annotation claim; relocate out-of-context taxonomy sentence
tldr_evidence: ISS-1728: §4.1 prose says "the audit table below annotates each" (λ_g-dependent rows) but the actual 28-row audit table has no per-row λ_g annotations in its cells. The "Shared Constant / Prior WIN" column lists static wins and borrowed values but does not mark λ_g dependency explicitly. Fix: change "annotates each" to "identifies the primary shared constants" to match what the table actually does. | ISS-1729: §4.1 TLDR includes "The author's April 2026 methodology.json defines a five-class claim taxonomy, but as of this writing it has been applied to only 9 of the site's 69+ claims" — this taxonomy point is about the methodology.json WIN-classification system and belongs in §6 or §8, not in a TLDR about the Live Power dashboard audit (28 scored domains, 12 knobs, 61.46σ). The sentence reads as a non-sequitur in the §4.1 context. Fix: remove from §4.1 TLDR. The claim appears in full in the §4.1 body (line ~3656) so no content is lost.

patches.description: Fix unsupported "annotates each" claim — table does not annotate per-row λ_g dependency (ISS-1728)
patches.find_pattern: (these three account for the majority of the 14 λ<sub>g</sub>-dependent rows identified in the knob count above; the audit table below annotates each).
patches.replace_pattern: (these three account for the majority of the 14 λ<sub>g</sub>-dependent rows identified in the knob count above; the audit table’s Shared Constant column identifies the primary shared constant for each row).

patches.description: Remove out-of-context methodology.json taxonomy sentence from §4.1 TLDR (ISS-1729)
patches.find_pattern: The author’s April 2026 methodology.json defines a five-class claim taxonomy, but as of this writing it has been applied to only 9 of the site’s 69+ claims; the taxonomy is a definition, not yet a broadly applied audit.
patches.note: The taxonomy analysis is preserved in full in the §4.1 body at line ~3656. Removing from TLDR eliminates a non-sequitur in a TLDR about dashboard mechanics. The TLDR sentence about "The 28-scored audit table below" that follows reads correctly without this removal.

tldr_verdict: Two prose precision fixes in §4.1: (1) "annotates each" changed to "identifies the primary shared constant" to match what the table actually shows; (2) taxonomy-status sentence removed from §4.1 TLDR where it is a non-sequitur (full text remains in body).
